overall_assessment.fls_score_context: 436.75 FLS represents solid intermediate performance - you're demonstrating proper technique fundamentals with room to optimize precision and efficiency.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Strong knot sequence execution with excellent timing, focus next on suture placement precision to eliminate deviation penalties.

rubric_insights.observation: 139 seconds completion time is efficient and well within competitive range
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Strong time performance contributed positively to overall score

rubric_insights.observation: Combined 3.25mm deviation from target marks cost significant points
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Lost 3.25 points due to placement accuracy - this is the primary score limiter

rubric_insights.observation: Proper surgeon's knot followed by alternating single throws with hand switching
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full knot technique points earned - no penalties for sequence or security

rubric_insights.observation: Complete slit closure achieved without gaps or avulsion
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full points earned for closure quality

technique_coaching.observation: In frames 3-5, needle entry appears slightly off-center from the black target marks, particularly visible at the second mark
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Precise suture placement is critical for optimal tissue approximation and directly impacts FLS scoring
technique_coaching.correction: Before needle entry, pause to align needle tip exactly perpendicular to the center of each black mark. Use the needle tip as a pointer to verify alignment before driving through.
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 needle placements on marked targets without completing the suture - focus only on perfect mark alignment and perpendicular entry angle

technique_coaching.observation: Frame 2 shows good needle loading technique with appropriate grasp point on the needle body
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Proper needle loading prevents wobble during driving and enables precise placement
technique_coaching.correction: Maintain this loading technique - you're grasping at the optimal 2/3 point from the tip
technique_coaching.drill: Continue current loading approach, add 10 practice loads focusing on consistent grasp point placement

technique_coaching.observation: Excellent hand switching visible between frames 4, 6, and 7 - proper alternating technique maintained throughout sequence
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Consistent hand switching prevents knot slippage and ensures proper square knot formation
technique_coaching.correction: No correction needed - maintain this excellent alternating pattern
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 15 knot sequences focusing on smooth hand transitions to build muscle memory

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frame 4 shows excellent surgeon's knot initiation with proper double-throw technique
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: The surgeon's knot provides superior initial security and prevents slippage during subsequent throws
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot sequence is textbook perfect - this fundamental technique will serve you well in advanced procedures

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frames 6-7 demonstrate consistent hand switching between throws, maintaining proper knot orientation
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: This alternating technique prevents knot slippage and ensures maximum holding strength
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your hand switching rhythm is excellent - this level of consistency shows strong motor learning

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Frame 21 shows complete drain closure with clean suture cut ends and no visible gaps
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Complete closure without gaps demonstrates proper knot tensioning and cutting technique
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your final result shows excellent attention to detail - the closure quality is exactly what we want to see

practice_plan.session_focus: Suture placement precision to eliminate deviation penalties
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5-minute needle loading practice - 30 repetitions focusing on consistent 2/3 grasp point

primary_drill.name: Target Precision Placement
primary_drill.description: 1) Position needle perpendicular to first mark, 2) Pause and verify alignment, 3) Drive through in single smooth motion, 4) Repeat for second mark, 5) Complete suture without focusing on speed
primary_drill.success_criteria: 8 out of 10 attempts with <1mm deviation from each target mark

practice_plan.cooldown: Review final knot position and assess placement accuracy relative to target marks
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 2-3 focused sessions should significantly reduce placement penalties

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early 28 FLS score - technique fundamentals now solid
progress_context.vs_peak: Close to your peak performance range (508-556 FLS) with better timing efficiency
progress_context.milestone_note: Target: Sub-2mm total deviation to break into 500+ FLS range consistently

coaching_limitations: Needle entry angle assessment limited by camera perspective - actual perpendicular alignment may be better than observed. Suture tension during knot tying not fully visible due to frame resolution.
